title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable social events for young people. The effort is a global expansion of Hinge's One More Hour social impact initiative aimed at addressing Gen Z loneliness.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles, and London.
Funds are for free or affordable social events for young people.
This is part of the global expansion of the One More Hour initiative.
The description links the initiative to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: Geographic scope is limited to NY, LA and London per the description — relevant locations to monitor for local event activity.
Description does not specify event scale, duration, or expected attendance, so no direct infrastructure impacts are stated.
No information in the description about energy, connectivity, or data needs associated with the events.
Decision-makers should note the initiative's presence in major urban areas and seek further details if assessing potential localized demand changes.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session shows how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent understands your business. The demo is end-to-end and requires no prior graph expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs give AI agents company-specific understanding that generic models lack.
The session builds a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents.
Connects the graph to an AI agent to enable business-aware responses.
No need to learn graph queries or graph theory.
End-to-end, hands-on demo of the full pipeline.
takeaways: A knowledge-graph-backed agent can make AI tools aware of your data center/grid operations and documentation.
Automated graph-building from raw documents reduces the need to hire or train specialists in graph queries.
An end-to-end approach can accelerate deployment of enterprise-specific AI agents for operational support.
Leverages existing internal documents to make AI agents relevant to your organization.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US explains that energy tax incentives are only part of the sustainability tax picture and highlights less obvious credits and incentives that can support sustainability initiatives. She points to state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation, and emphasizes that viewing sustainability investments more broadly can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only sustainability-related tax opportunities.
State and local abatements and location-based incentives can support sustainability projects.
R&D tax credits may apply to innovation efforts, including reimagining processes (and similar improvements).
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can create significant tax value.
takeaways: Don’t limit evaluations to energy credits — consider state/local abatements and location-based incentives when siting or upgrading grid and data center assets.
Assess innovation-focused projects (process or design improvements) for potential R&D tax credit eligibility.
Incorporate tax incentive analysis into sustainability project planning to capture additional value.
Work with tax advisors to identify and document less obvious incentives relevant to infrastructure and operational changes.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video uses a star metaphor — "the sky is full of stars" — to show Copilot helping Christa find her "inner star." It invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Uses a star metaphor: 'the sky is full of stars' and finding the 'star within.'
Centers on Christa as the person helped by Copilot.
Direct call to action: 'Download & try Copilot' via link in bio.
Uplifting, concise promo tone with a star emoji 🌟
takeaways: Marketing positions Copilot as a personal, user-facing tool — expect user interest that may affect support needs.
The explicit CTA (link in bio) can drive immediate downloads — consider capacity and monitoring readiness.
Emotional, simple messaging may drive adoption; ensure onboarding and user guidance are available.
Short promotional content can create traffic spikes; verify scalability and security posture ahead of campaigns.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest: u/JeffersonPutnam, whose winning entry is a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The description credits host Dwight and provides a full PCPartPicker parts list and detailed system specs.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam from the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest
Build: small-form-factor (Fractal Design Era 2) GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition rig
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato)
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker: https://pcpartpicker.com/list/hxnCRV
Key components listed: MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS
takeaways: The build uses a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition in a compact chassis, underscoring power and cooling needs when deploying high-end consumer GPUs in small-form-factor systems.

A Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU and a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO are used to support the RTX 5090, highlighting the need to consider high-wattage SFX power and liquid cooling for compact, high-performance nodes.
Pairing a Ryzen 7 9800X3D with 32GB DDR5-6000 memory reflects modern single-node CPU/memory configurations for demanding desktop workloads.
The provided PCPartPicker list offers a clear bill of materials for replication and component sourcing.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI-powered solutions that unlocked new revenue streams. The project leveraged Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline, and expand what the business considered possible.
bullets: TTEC collaborated with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were core to the solution.
The effort opened entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Operations were streamlined and developer productivity improved.
Consulting support compressed the digital transformation timeline and expanded ambitions.
takeaways: Partnering with cloud consulting can speed AI deployment and shorten transformation timelines.
Robust cloud infrastructure is key for running GenAI workloads and accelerating prototyping.
Rapid prototyping can directly enable new revenue opportunities—allocate resources accordingly.
Investing in developer productivity tools and cloud services can improve operational efficiency.
